step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the initial assessment that enlargement of the blind spot is associated with primary open-angle glaucoma. One additional point supporting this is that the loss of retinal ganglion cell axons leads to structural changes within the optic disc, contributing to an apparent increase in the cup-to-disc ratio, which can be observed through ophthalmoscopic examination. Furthermore, the progressive nature of glaucomatous damage often results in more pronounced changes such as a larger physiologic blind spot, making it consistent across patients under similar disease stages. Therefore:

My answer remains **A**.
```
```markdown. I agree with the initial assessment that enlargement of the blind spot is associated with primary open-angle glaucoma. An important addition to support this is the fact that chronic elevation of intraocular pressure damages retinal ganglion cells' axonal pathways, particularly those involved in transmitting signals related to peripheral vision, thereby enlarging the region where no light stimuli register—essentially increasing the blind spot's perceived area. Given this detailed mechanism, my final answer remains **A**.
```
